summary: I was born and raised in the province of Cebu in the Philippines, specifically in Guanbacil. Growing up there with my parents, my life was simple and ordinary. Cebu holds a special place in my heart because it is where my roots are and where my family still lives.

The Philippines is my homeland where I grew up and where my family still resides. It was in the Philippines that I met my future husband through Yahoo! Chat, and he visited me there in September 2006. Before moving to the United States in 2008, I traveled around the Philippines with my parents, including a memorable trip on the SuperFerry to the capital for my visa interview and medical exams.

I came to the United States on July 19, 2008, to be with my husband and to seek a better future. After arriving, we got married soon after, and I eventually became a U.S. citizen. Living here has allowed me to have a stable job as a certified manager and support my family, which I am very thankful for.

I met my future husband on Yahoo! Chat, which was how we first connected despite being from different countries. This online platform brought us together before he visited me in the Philippines in September 2006. Yahoo! Chat played a crucial role in starting our relationship that eventually led to marriage.

When I moved to the United States, I settled in Minnesota, leaving my family and friends behind in the Philippines. Minnesota became my new home because my husband lives here, and it is where I have built my life. Living in Minnesota has given me the opportunity to grow personally and professionally.

Currently, I live in West St. Paul, Minnesota, where my husband resides. This place is important to me because it represents the life we have built together after I moved here in 2008. West St. Paul is where I feel at home now, surrounded by the love and support of my husband.

I remember traveling on the SuperFerry with my parents to the capital of the Philippines after my husband left. This trip was significant because I was going for my visa interview and medical examinations. The SuperFerry journey reminds me of my parents' support and the strength they gave me during that challenging time.
